result.summary: Recorded Make’s developer integration surface: one zone-based REST API, one cloud MCP server (OAuth or token-authenticated), and the Make CLI; no first-party GraphQL API surface found.

make_api_token.label: Make API token / API key
make_api_token.setup: In Make, open your profile and create a token from the API area: follow [Creating API token](https://developers.make.com/api-documentation/authentication/create-authentication-token). The docs say to sign in, open **Profile**, open the **API** tab, click **Add token**, choose a label and scopes, then save and copy the token. For CLI use, the login wizard may open the API key page in your browser and ask you to paste the key; see [Authenticate the Make CLI](https://developers.make.com/make-cli/make-cli/authenticate-the-make-cli). For MCP token usage, Make says to open **Profile** → **API access** → **Tokens** → **Add token** and include the `mcp:use` scope if you want scenarios available as tools; see [Connect using MCP token](https://developers.make.com/mcp-server/connect-using-mcp-token).

make_oauth_client.label: Make OAuth 2.0 client credentials
make_oauth_client.setup: Request OAuth client credentials from Make by submitting the [OAuth client registration form](https://f.make.com/oauth) as described in [Requesting an OAuth 2.0 client](https://developers.make.com/api-documentation/authentication/requesting-an-oauth-2.0-client). Choose the API scopes you need and wait for approval; the docs say approved apps receive the credentials needed to integrate. For MCP OAuth, Make also exposes OAuth endpoints at `https://www.make.com/oauth/v2/authorize` and `https://www.make.com/oauth/v2/token`, and dynamic client registration at `https://www.make.com/oauth/v2/register/mcp`.

variables.name: zone_url
variables.description: The docs say requests are sent to your Make zone, for example `https://eu1.make.com`.
